title: agent_families.yaml
description: Schema for the agent families registry. Each family declares (a) launch contract — bin / version_flag / supports / incompatibilities — and (b) optional frame_profile — declarative stream-json translation rules per ADR-010. Editor LSPs use this for autocomplete + inline validation; AI agent maintainers use it as a structural reference alongside docs/reference/frame-profiles.md.

family.description: Wire-level family name. Used in hosts.capabilities_json, template backend.kind, audit rows, and as the overlay-file basename. Lowercase-hyphenated.

version_flag.description: Argument the probe runs to read a version string. Empty/omitted skips the version probe.

supports.description: Mode tokens this family is known to support; consumed by modes.Resolve to reject incompatible spawn requests.

frame_profile.description: Per-engine declarative translator for stream-json frames. Optional in v1; nil falls back to the legacy hardcoded translator (driver_stdio.go).

frame_translator.description: Selects which translator the host-runner uses. Empty/'legacy' (default) → hardcoded translate(); 'profile' → ApplyProfile authoritative; 'both' → profile authoritative + legacy shadow with divergence logging (ADR-010 Phase 1.6 canary).

description.description: Agent-facing prose header. ~3 lines stating dispatch semantics + scope conventions inline so a fresh maintainer reading rule 17 sees the model. Optional but strongly recommended.

profile_version.description: Schema version. Loaders accept lower versions when forward-compatible; unknown higher versions are rejected so old hubs don't silently misbehave on a new overlay.

match.description: AND-ed top-level field equality. Empty match (or absent) matches any frame at lowest specificity. Specificity = number of keys; most-specific match wins, ties fire in declaration order.

for_each.description: Optional. Expression yielding an array; the rule fires once per element with that element as inner scope ($.) and the parent frame as outer scope ($$.).

when_present.description: Optional. Gates the emit on a non-nil expression evaluation. Used so usage events don't fire as all-nils when the SDK omits message.usage. A gated rule does not trigger the raw fallback.

sub_rules.description: Only meaningful with for_each. Per-element dispatch on inner-scope shape (e.g. content[].type ∈ {text, tool_use}). First sub-rule whose match is satisfied fires.

kind.description: agent_event kind column. Reuse existing kinds where possible (text, tool_call, tool_result, usage, rate_limit, session.init, system, error, raw, …); a new kind requires mobile renderer support.

producer.description: Producer column. Default 'agent'. Override for system-originated frames to 'system' or for hub-side rules to 'hub'.

payload.description: Map of output field → expression. Expressions are evaluated in the rule's scope (inner if inside for_each, outer via $$.) and resolved to the agent_event row's payload_json. Mutually exclusive with payload_expr.

payload_expr.description: Single expression yielding the *entire* payload (whole-payload passthrough). Used when the legacy translator passes the raw frame as payload — system fallback, error, deprecated completion alias. Must resolve to a map; non-map values yield an empty payload defensively. Mutually exclusive with payload (payload_expr wins if both are set).
